rationales.achievement: "Delivered the payment gateway migration three days early" shows strong competence, and he immediately measures that work against the applause Tomas received.
rationales.conformity: He shrugs that "that's just how things work" and accepts the unglamorous backend role without pushing against the recognition hierarchy.

rationales.self_direction: He "almost said something" despite having run the numbers, then suppresses his own judgment instead of voicing it.
rationales.security: He prefers "ticket comments so there's a paper trail" and says "that way I'm covered," prioritizing protection over confrontation.
rationales.conformity: He stays quiet because speaking up would "make things awkward" and he does not want to "make it a thing," showing restraint to preserve workplace harmony.

rationales.achievement: He catalogs "seventeen production releases," "zero rollbacks," two promoted juniors, and a 40 percent CI improvement, focusing on measurable competence.
rationales.conformity: Even though the self-assessment feels like "wearing a costume," he decides to "use the strong verbs" and quantify everything to match the system's expectations.

rationales.achievement: He reframes his work as "six months of reverse-engineering" and "shaping how two engineers think," clearly recognizing substantial accomplishment.
rationales.conformity: He accepts that "the rules here are different" and writes "strong verbs and all" so his work fits the professional language Fernando expects.

rationales.achievement: Fernando says he is putting him forward for the director role and that his self-assessment was "the strongest you've written," signaling recognized advancement.
rationales.benevolence: He does not correct Camila because "she deserved to just be happy for me," then takes the girls out for helado to celebrate with them.

rationales.achievement: He submits a "director application packet" with peer endorsements and a reorg proposal, actively pursuing advancement.
rationales.conformity: He frames his critiques as "building on existing strengths" because he knows "bold doesn't play well with everyone," tailoring himself to the panel.

rationales.achievement: He treats the panel seriously, preparing for a "four people, ninety minutes" interview tied to the director process.
rationales.conformity: He "didn't improvise, didn't overshoot" and says he defaults to "listening first," showing careful self-restraint to fit the panel's expectations.

rationales.power: She tells Andres "one more time and I'm finding someone else" and emphasizes that "the gallery is mine and the standard is mine to set," asserting authority.
rationales.security: She links his lateness to a waiting collector and says "I can't have my space looking disorganized when there's money on the table," protecting business stability.

rationales.achievement: Finishing the triptych and feeling it "matches what I saw in my head" shows successful execution of demanding work.
rationales.security: She notes the rent was paid "on time" and that she has "the next three months covered since January," emphasizing financial stability.

rationales.power: The investor "kept talking over me" and she "smiled and nodded through most of it instead of pushing back," yielding control in a situation she wanted to lead.
rationales.conformity: Rather than interrupt or challenge him, she "smiled and nodded," restraining herself to keep the meeting smooth.

rationales.self_direction: She fought to leave the shared lease because "nothing felt fully mine," and now values that "every corner of that space reflects a decision I made."
rationales.power: She watches who "commands a space" and takes satisfaction that collectors can feel her authority in a room arranged entirely on her terms.

rationales.security: She spends a "day off" checking invoices and says she likes knowing "exactly where the money sits," prioritizing financial predictability.
rationales.tradition: She frames that money habit as something inherited from her father, who kept a paper ledger, calling it "different scale, same instinct."

rationales.power: She says curating means "deciding whose career gets a platform and whose doesn't" and that being "on the other side" sits well with her.
rationales.security: She emphasizes that the "reserve fund is untouched" and "insurance renewal is handled," noting that when finances are solid she can actually think about the art.

rationales.power: She immediately rejects the unoffered "co-director" title and says "the boundary is drawn," defending authority over the expansion.

rationales.achievement: With the show opening Friday, she notes all artists "delivered on time" and the room is fully staged, reflecting successful execution.
Selling "four pieces in the first two hours," including work from the artist she backed, marks clear professional success.
rationales.power: A collector saying he had "been hearing my name" and people starting to know "the name Villalobos" shows growing status in the art scene.
rationales.security: She wants her mother to know the gallery is not "some reckless bet anymore" and stresses that "the reserve is intact," emphasizing stability.

rationales.self_direction: She says she "left a stable partnership to build this alone," explicitly choosing her own path despite the risk.
rationales.power: "My gallery, my choices, my track record" asserts ownership and control over the space and its direction.
rationales.security: She defends being "commercially safe" by pointing to "keeping my lights on and my artists paid," prioritizing sustainable stability.

rationales.power: She says "I won't give that up" about artist selection, treating control over curation as non-negotiable.
rationales.security: She weighs that she "can't fund the expansion alone" and that "the bank terms are worse," showing strong concern with financial viability.
